course_description:
Candidates will research and write an original thesis on a topic in communication relevant to their field of study. Typically candidates complete this course in their last semester. To enroll in the course please contact the School of Communication and Arts (student.commarts@uq.edu.au).

This course offers project management skills to assist you with the production of your thesis/project. It is the culmination of your Master of Communication program. The course offers you the chance to conduct original research on a topic relevant to your elected field of study. You will individually carry out original research in a field or fields of communication.

Throughout the course you will work with an academic advisor from the School of Communication and Arts. You are responsible for selecting and approaching an advisor, and you should arrange this before or during the first week of semester.

This course gives you the opportunity to engage in reflective communication practice or add to the research literature through an in-depth study of a topic of your choice. The course offers two options:

Option 1: Thesis that reports an original communication research project.

Scope: The following approaches are considered suitable for a 12,000 word thesis:

Option 2: Communication project and exegesis (6,000 words).

Scope: Examples of suitable communication projects include:

The selection of your preferred option must be made at the start of semester in consultation with your academic advisor.

Structured workshops will be offered throughout the semester to assist with the progress of your project.

Ethical Clearance, Risk Assessment and Travel Advice

If your thesis or project will involve any interaction/data collection with human subjects (online or face-to-face) AND you plan to PUBLISH any data from your thesis in the future, you will need to apply for and receive ethical clearance via MyResearch site, BEFORE you commence your fieldwork.

This can be a fairly time-consuming process so if you need to undergo this step, please contact the course coordinator at the earliest (approval can take a few weeks and this needs to be factored into your submission timeline).ﾠ

Please visit http://www.uq.edu.au/research/integrity-compliance/low-and-negligible-risk-reviews and see Section titled 'Does a student assignment or activity require research ethics review?' to understand which student project needs ethical clearance, and which does not.

If your research project involves local travel to collect data, you must undertake a risk assessment. You must complete the online risk assessment form as well as the UQ online Travel Form. Please note that if you plan to travel verseas for purposes of collecting data for your thesis, you are required to check the travel advice provided by Smartraveller: The Australian Government Travel Advisory and Consular Assistance Service ( http://www.smartraveller.gov.au/ ).

incompatible: You can't enrol in this course if you've already completed the following:
restrictions: Required cumulative GPA of 6.0 and required grade of 5.0 in a postgraduate research methods course. Availability of a supervisor and Course Coordinator/Program Convenor approval is required to enrol in this course.

timetable:
The timetable for this course is available on the
UQ Public Timetable
.
Additional timetable information
A total of six workshops (2 hours each) are scheduled during the semester in Weeks 1, 2, 3, 6, 8 & 11. Please make sure that you are familiar with the workshop schedule (see Learning Activities in the Course Profile). The first workshop commences in Week 1. Please check Allocate+ regularly for more logistical information.
In addition to these workshops, you are expected to arrange weekly/fortnightly meetings with your supervisor (these are part of milestone requirements to pass the course - see under Assessment).ﾠ I am available for student consultation by appointment.
course_aims:
This course gives you the opportunity to undertake an original, in-depth research thesis, or engage in a critically reflective communication project on a relevant and approved communication topic.The course offers two options:

The selection of your preferred option must be made at the start of the semester, and in consultation with your academic advisor.

learning_outcomes.description: Theorise by designing, positioning and justifying a project that addresses gaps within your specified field of practice or research.
Critically engage in or contribute to the understandings of communication research and/or practice.
Evaluate and use appropriate and ethical methodologies to complete your project.
Position your project within the literature relevant to your project.
Communicate complex ideas clearly and fluently, using conventions relevant to your topic.
Critically reflect on your project and its contribution to contemporary communication practice/s and/or disciplinary knowledge.
Work independently and critically reflect on your capacity to assume leadership in the field of communication research or practice.

assessment_summary.category: Paper/ Report/ Annotation, Participation/ Student contribution

learning_resources.library_resources: Library resources are available on the UQ Library website .

learning_activities.topic: Week 1: Course Orientation This workshop provides general information to students undertaking the thesis in SCA. It canvasses what is expected of you. It will introduce you to the key elements of, and stages in managing your research project, and how these relate to the assessment criteria. By the end of Week 4 you must submit an outline of your proposal. Learning outcomes: L01

Week 2: Research Proposal & Ethics The workshop will look at strategies for developing a research proposal which is due Week 4. We will also discuss the ethics that inform decisions at every step of the research process: choice of research topic, choice of methods, data collection strategies, analysis and reporting. This is important to know especially for those of you who need to ASAP apply for ethics as part of this course if you have not done so already. Work on refining research question/s and overall project design. Learning outcomes: L01, L03, L04

Week 3: Lit Review and theory Your individual proposal is due next week. This week, you will be introduced to guidelines on how review the literature in your area of study, define a theoretical framework and ensure both are linked to RQ/s. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04

Week 6: Research methods, data analysis, and writing workshop This workshop will revisit various research methods and software for qualitative analyses. GUEST LECTURE by ELSO Dr. Hojjat on writing. Learning outcomes: L03, L05

Week 8: Doing data analysis Data Analysis: This workshop will be a hands-on exercise in how to come up with themes from qualitative data. Readings/Ref: Weerakkody ; Wadsworth Learning outcomes: L03, L05, L06

Week 11: Production of final thesis & Writing exercise This final session focuses on the production of your final thesis and submission due end of Week 13. You should bring your near-complete project doc with you and be prepared to discuss it with your fellow students. There will be a second writing workshop as well. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06, L07

policies_and_procedures: University policies and procedures apply to all aspects of student life. As a UQ student, you must comply with University-wide and program-specific requirements, including the: Student Code of Conduct Policy Student Integrity and Misconduct Policy and Procedure Assessment Procedure Examinations Procedure Reasonable Adjustments for Students Policy and Procedure AI for Assessment Guide Recording of Teaching Policy and Procedure Learn more about UQ policies on my.UQ and the Policy and Procedure Library . Course guidelines Communication Expectations While you are a student at UQ, all communication must be conducted according to the UQ Student Code of Conduct . The UQ Library has a helpful Communicate and collaborate online module . Email is the primary way for you to send messages to, and receive information from, the School and our staff. You must use your UQ email address (not a private address) to communicate with staff. You should add a clear subject line, including course code, and a 2-3 word statement. You can send email at any time, however, please do not expect responses outside normal working hours (Monday to Friday from ~8am to ~5pm). Emails that constitute bullying, harassment or discrimination against staff contravene the Student Code of Conduct. Emails like this will be reported to the University, and the matter will be pursued as misconduct. Learning activities
